summary: The frontend container follows a component‑based Angular architecture with clear presentation, business, and integration layers, leveraging TypeScript, RxJS and the Angular CLI stack to expose a rich set of deed‑registry capabilities through REST adapters. While functional coverage is comprehensive, quality metrics reveal moderate separation of concerns, coupling and cohesion, and a high technical debt signal from the unusually large number of services, adapters and pipes, indicating opportunities to tighten abstractions and module boundaries.

layers: Presentation Layer (Components, Directives, Pipes, UI Modules)
Integration/Data Access Layer (Adapters, API Modules, HTTP Interceptors)
technical.integration_style: REST API (HTTP calls via services and adapters)

capabilities: Create, edit, view and delete deeds
Manage deed workflows (archive, finalize, change AOID, delete)
Perform reencryption and related background jobs
Configure application settings and user preferences
Interact with backend services via REST adapters
functional.bounded_context: Deed Registry and Legal Document Management
integrations: Various REST endpoints defined in the interface list (e.g., /deed-entries, /document, /participants, /uvz, /access-denied, etc.)

debt_indicators: Very high number of services (131) – likely some are oversized or overlapping
Large number of adapters (50) – may indicate missing higher‑level abstraction or façade
Excessive pipe count (67) – potential duplication of transformation logic
No explicit relation mapping – hard to verify proper module boundaries
recommendations: Identify and split large services into smaller, domain‑focused services
Introduce façade or gateway services to reduce direct component‑to‑adapter coupling
Consolidate similar pipes into shared utility functions or a limited set of reusable pipes
Review module imports/exports to enforce strict feature module boundaries
Add interface abstractions for adapters and services to ease testing and future replacements
Implement a linting rule set (e.g., ESLint with Angular‑specific rules) to detect cyclic dependencies and overly deep injection hierarchies

overall_assessment: The frontend delivers robust functional coverage for deed‑registry workflows using a solid Angular foundation, yet architectural drift—evident from the proliferation of services, adapters and pipes—creates maintainability risk. Addressing these quality concerns will improve modularity, testability and long‑term scalability.
top_recommendations: Consolidate and refactor oversized services into focused, domain‑specific units.
Introduce façade/gateway layers to decouple components from low‑level adapters.
Enforce strict feature‑module boundaries and clean up duplicate pipes.

layers: Presentation Layer (Components, Directives, Pipes, UI Modules)
Integration/Data Access Layer (Adapters, API Modules, HTTP Interceptors)
